scene.description: A photorealistic dusk blue-hour exterior of the completed Anna's Farm Site One common-space entry landscape. The sky is a deep cobalt with a fading warm band at the horizon. All ten solar path lights are fully glowing warm 2700K amber, casting soft pools of light across the decomposed granite and rimming the boulder bases. The dry-riverbed accent line reads as a darker channel; flagstone clusters catch faint specular reflections from the path lights. Subtle ambient fill from a distant streetlamp off-frame.
scene.location: Lafayette, Colorado, Boulder County (40.0004 N, -105.110 W)

materials.type: tan decomposed granite, cool blue-shadowed with warm pools near lights
rust-ochre rip-rap, reading dark against warm light pools
weathered sandstone, warm rim-light from path lights
buff Lyons sandstone flagstone, catching subtle specular highlights
matte-black solar path lights, fully glowing warm 2700K amber with visible filament, subtle lens bloom

lighting.source: ambient blue-hour skylight plus ten warm practical path lights
lighting.direction: omnidirectional blue fill plus localized warm pools
lighting.practical_lights: solar path lights at full glow, warm 2700K, gentle bloom, light pooling on granite

camera.framing: same 3/4 angle as hero_golden, slightly tighter on the light curve

composition.foreground: warm light pool on decomposed granite, first two path lights
composition.midground: curve of glowing lights leading to hero boulder cluster
composition.background: silhouetted stucco wall against cobalt sky

style.reference: Architectural Digest dusk exterior, real-estate twilight shot

include: warm 2700K light pools with soft falloff
cool blue ambient fill from sky
